identity.short_name: GCD -> discriminant -> digit sum -> finite-field root count of x^{2T+1}-x

subdomain(s): affine-linear constraint counting over finite fields

problem_spec.summary: Composite (CHAIN) family: compute bridge quantity T from component A (GCD  discriminant digit sum  engineered (2+1) derivative  splitting field  log modulus); then use that T as a required parameter in component B (Count F13 degree-8 twisted-reciprocal polynomials with exactly two inversion 2-cycles as roots) to determine the final output (reduced mod 1000). Instance summary: Compute a gcd to obtain a quadratic, take its discriminant and digit sum T, then count roots of x^{2T+1}-x over F29 using cyclic multiplicative group structure; output mod 1000.
givens: D=Disc(F); T is digit sum of |D|.
ask: Compute number of roots of h in F_29 mod 1000.

parameter(s).name: P_coeffs
parameter(s).domain: integer coefficient list defining a degree-6 polynomial in [x]
parameter(s).purpose: determines the gcd F and hence δ and γ

parameter(s).name: Q_coeffs
parameter(s).domain: integer coefficient list defining a degree-6 polynomial in [x]
parameter(s).purpose: determines the gcd F and hence δ and γ

parameter(s).name: base_prime_p
parameter(s).purpose: sets characteristic for the formal derivative and determines finite-field sizes p^d

parameter(s).name: exponent_template
parameter(s).domain: f(x)=x^{pδ}+c x^{2γ+1}-x with c=(2γ+1)^{-1} in F_p
parameter(s).purpose: forces derivative cancellation of the pδ term and normalizes the x^{2γ} coefficient to 1

parameter(s).name: log_base
parameter(s).purpose: fixes the logarithm base so log_{p^2}(p^d)=d/2 and 2 \cdot log_{p^2}(m)=d is an integer

parameter(s).name: q
parameter(s).purpose: determines F_q^* cyclic structure and inversion orbits; instance uses q=13.

parameter(s).name: twist_exponent_k
parameter(s).purpose: defines twisted reciprocity f(x)=x^k f(1/x) on F_q^*; instance uses k=4.

parameter(s).name: fixed_coefficient
parameter(s).purpose: pins a coefficient in the reduced template; instance fixes a3=5.

parameter(s).name: root_count_target
parameter(s).purpose: target number of distinct roots in F_q^*; instance uses 4.

parameter(s).name: fixed_point_exclusion
parameter(s).purpose: forces roots to come only from inversion 2-cycles, not fixed points.

parameter(s).name: T_bridge
parameter(s).domain: derived integer computed from component A invariants
parameter(s).purpose: interface quantity produced by component A and consumed by component B (non-stapled coupling).

invariant(s).name: gcd_quadratic_invariant
invariant(s).statement: F=gcd(P,Q) is exactly the shared quadratic factor.

invariant(s).name: gcd_uniqueness_in_Zx
invariant(s).statement: F(x)=gcd(P,Q) is uniquely determined up to ±1, fixed by requiring positive leading coefficient.

invariant(s).name: discriminant_is_integer
invariant(s).statement: For quadratic F(x)=ax^2+bx+c in [x], =b^2-4ac is an integer determined by coefficients.

invariant(s).name: derivative_cancellation_in_char_p
invariant(s).statement: In characteristic p, (x^{pδ})' = pδ \cdot x^{pδ-1} = 0, so the x^{pδ} term vanishes in f'(x).

invariant(s).name: engineered_inverse_for_unit_x_2gamma_term
invariant(s).statement: With c=(2γ+1)^{-1} in _p, we have (c x^{2γ+1})' = c(2γ+1)x^{2γ} = x^{2γ}, forcing f'(x)=x^{2γ}-1.

invariant(s).name: splitting_of_xn_minus_1
invariant(s).statement: If gcd(p,2γ)=1, then x^{2γ}-1 splits over _{p^d} iff 2γ | (p^d-1). The minimal such d is ord_{2γ}(p).

invariant(s).name: log_p2_prime_power
invariant(s).statement: If m=p^d then log_{p^2}(m)=d/2, hence 2 \cdot log_{p^2}(m)=d is an integer.

invariant(s).name: coefficient_template_from_twisted_symmetry
invariant(s).statement: Using x^(q-1)=1 on F_q^* and comparing reduced coefficients modulo x^(q-1)-1, the condition f(x)=x^4 f(1/x) on F_13^* forces a5=a6=a7=0 and a4=a0, a3=a1, leaving the template f(x)=x^8 + a0 x^4 + a1 x^3 + a2 x^2 + a1 x + a0.

invariant(s).name: parameter_reduction_by_fixed_coefficient
invariant(s).statement: Fixing the x^3 coefficient a3=5 forces a1=5, so the family is parameterized by (a0,a2)F_13^2.

invariant(s).name: inversion_orbits_in_Fq_star
invariant(s).statement: Inversion partitions F_13^* into fixed points {1,-1} and five 2-cycles: {2,7},{3,9},{4,10},{5,8},{6,11}.

invariant(s).name: root_orbit_closure_under_twisted_symmetry
invariant(s).statement: If rF_13^* and f(r)=0, then 0=f(r)=r^4 f(1/r), so f(1/r)=0. Thus roots occur in inversion-orbits.

invariant(s).name: exact_root_count_to_orbit_selection
invariant(s).statement: With fixed points excluded and root_count_target=4, the root set in F_13^* must consist of exactly two inversion 2-cycles (and no other orbit).

invariant(s).name: orbit_root_conditions_are_affine_lines
invariant(s).statement: For each 2-cycle representative r, the condition f(r)=0 is an affine-linear equation in (a0,a2), i.e. a line in F_13^2. Thus "exactly two 2-cycles are roots corresponds to points lying on exactly two of the five orbit-lines and not on the fixed-point root lines.

invariant(s).name: bridge_interface_invariant
invariant(s).statement: The composite is coupled by the interface variable T: component A uniquely determines T, and component B is defined/solved only after substituting this specific T into its parameter slot.

essential_assumption(s): p is prime so F_p^* is cyclic; roots are counted in F_29 only.
The computed gcd F(x) has degree 2 (quadratic).
2γ+1 is nonzero in _p (equivalently p ∤ (2γ+1)), so c=(2γ+1)^{-1} exists.
gcd(p,2γ)=1 so x^{2γ}-1 has distinct roots and the roots-of-unity splitting criterion applies cleanly.
m is taken among finite fields _{p^d} (prime-power sizes).
The symmetry f(x)=x^4 f(1/x) is required only on F_13^*, not as a polynomial identity in F_13[x].
Roots counted are distinct and lie in F_13^* (x=0 excluded).
Fixed-point exclusion f(1)0 is enforced as part of the specification.
The bridge quantity T computed from component A is guaranteed to be an integer in the admissible range required by component B (e.g., as an index, bound, or group-action parameter).
uniqueness_certificate(s): gcd and discriminant are deterministic; digit sum gives unique T.
Cyclic-group counting yields a unique root count for h.

F is uniquely determined as the gcd in ℤ[x] with positive leading coefficient, hence δ and γ are deterministic. The coefficient c=(2γ+1)^{-1} is unique in _p, so f' is uniquely x^{2γ}-1. The smallest extension degree d for splitting is uniquely ord_{2γ}(p), hence m=p^d is unique. Since 2 \cdot log_{p^2}(m)=d and γ are fixed, the modulus (d+γ) and the residue δ mod (d+γ) are uniquely determined.

The symmetry and fixed coefficient reduce f to a finite 2-parameter family (a0,a2)F_13^2. Each inversion 2-cycle root condition is a single affine-linear constraint in these parameters. Therefore the valid polynomials correspond to a finite, explicitly enumerable set of parameter points, uniquely determining the count.

Because every element of F_13^* lies in exactly one inversion-orbit, verifying that a parameter point lies on exactly two orbit-lines (and on neither fixed-point line) is equivalent to verifying the exact root-orbit pattern; hence correctness is checkable and the answer is unique.
(N1 necessity) If T is not computed from component A, component B has multiple admissible outputs across different possible parameter choices; thus the composite is ill-posed without the bridge.
(N2 non-factorization) The final answer is not a trivial Combine(ansA, ansB): the computed T restricts/changes the admissible branch structure inside component B before its output is even defined.

technique(s).name: finite_field_cyclic_group_root_count
technique(s).purpose: count solutions by gcd with p-1

technique(s).name: compute_gcd_by_structured_difference
technique(s).purpose: compute F=gcd(P,Q) using that gcd divides PQ and a visible common quadratic factor

technique(s).name: digit_sum_evaluation
technique(s).purpose: compute γ as the sum of decimal digits of δ

technique(s).name: invert_scalar_in_Fp
technique(s).purpose: compute c=(2γ+1)^{-1} to normalize the derivative coefficient

technique(s).name: formal_derivative_engineering_to_x_2gamma_minus_1
technique(s).purpose: use characteristic-p cancellation and inverse normalization to force f'(x)=x^{2γ}-1

technique(s).name: minimal_splitting_field_for_xn_minus_1
technique(s).purpose: find minimal d such that n | (p^d-1), i.e. d=ord_n(p), so m=p^d

technique(s).name: evaluate_logarithm_of_prime_power_and_reduce_modulus
technique(s).purpose: compute 2 \cdot log_{p^2}(m)=d and then reduce δ modulo d+γ

technique(s).name: convert_pointwise_symmetry_to_coefficient_constraints
inputs: twisted symmetry f(x)=x^4 f(1/x) on F_13^*
technique(s).purpose: deduce relations among coefficients using reduction modulo x^(q-1)-1.
outputs: reduced coefficient template with few free parameters

technique(s).name: describe_inversion_orbits
technique(s).purpose: list fixed points and 2-cycles under inversion.
outputs: orbit decomposition used for root counting

technique(s).name: translate_root_conditions_to_affine_linear_constraints
inputs: reduced template and orbit representative r
technique(s).purpose: impose "orbit is a root by evaluating f(r)=0.
outputs: one affine-linear equation (a line) per orbit in parameter space

technique(s).name: intersection_and_parallelism_casework
technique(s).purpose: determine which orbit pairs can co-occur as roots and enumerate their intersections.
outputs: finite list of candidate parameter points

technique(s).name: enforce_exactly_two_orbits_and_exclude_fixed_points
technique(s).purpose: filter points that would create extra roots (third orbit or ±1 roots).

theorem(s).name: F_p_multiplicative_group_cyclic
theorem(s).role: F_p^* is cyclic of order p-1

theorem(s).name: Euclidean_algorithm_in_Zx
theorem(s).role: defines and guarantees uniqueness of gcd up to units in [x]

theorem(s).name: formal_derivative_in_characteristic_p
theorem(s).role: (x^{pk})' = 0 and (x^n)' = n x^{n-1} with coefficients reduced mod p

theorem(s).name: roots_of_unity_splitting_criterion
theorem(s).role: x^n-1 splits over _q iff n | (q-1) when gcd(n,char)=1

theorem(s).name: multiplicative_order_mod_n
theorem(s).role: minimal d with p^d  1 (mod n) equals ord_n(p)

theorem(s).name: Fermat little theorem / cyclicity of F_q^*
theorem(s).role: use x^(q-1)=1 to reduce exponents and convert 1/x into x^(q-2).

theorem(s).name: uniqueness_of_reduced_representative_mod_x^(q-1)_minus_1
theorem(s).role: equality of functions on F_q^* corresponds to equality of reduced coefficients in degrees 0..q-2.

theorem(s).name: linear algebra over finite fields
theorem(s).role: solving 22 affine-linear systems counts line intersections and detects parallel lines.

closest_first_principle_concept_applied: Finite-field multiplicative group structure (x^(q-1)=1) and orbit decomposition under inversion.

critical_trick(s): Extract the shared gcd factor and compute its discriminant.
Use digit sum to create an exponent parameter T.
Count finite-field roots using cyclic group order and gcd(2T,p-1).
Bridge Lemma (explicit): compute T in component A, then substitute this specific T into component B; justify N1 and N2 to rule out stapling.

library(s).name: math
library(s).reason: basic modular arithmetic and combinatorial counts

library(s).name: sage
library(s).reason: optional finite-field polynomial evaluation and root counting for independent verification.

import_hint(s): from sympy import symbols, Poly, gcd, discriminant, GF

task(s).name: primary_structural_check
task(s).reason: Execute the intended structural route in a CAS-friendly way: compute bridge T from component A using symbolic identities or explicit enumeration, then compute component B output with the substituted T, finally reduce mod 1000; confirm matches the recorded Answer.

task(s).name: secondary_independent_check
task(s).reason: Independent cross-check: avoid reusing the same derivation as primary. Use a different route (e.g., brute-force enumeration over the finite search space, numeric root approximation with direct evaluation, or an alternate invariant/Burnside count) to recompute the final answer and compare.
